steps.text: Initiate an intralaboratory method validation study.
Initiate an interlaboratory method validation study.

preconditions.text: Ensure a detailed procedure has been written, operational parameters are believed to be optimized and finalized, and no additional changes are anticipated.
Ensure a successful intralaboratory study has been completed.

constraints.text: If procedural changes are made during troubleshooting, some or all performance tests may need to be repeated.
If procedural changes are required after initiating a multiple laboratory study, some or all aspects of the interlaboratory study may need to be repeated.

steps.text: Clearly describe the measurement process components subjected to validation.
Include an evaluation of all combined measurement process components in the validation effort.
Clearly and completely state the measurement process components validated and the combination of procedures composing a method.
Clearly define the purpose of the method and the intended use of the data.
Well define and clearly describe the method scope and applicability, including validated components, analytes/matrices, range of analyte levels, limitations/assumptions, and DQO alignment.
Evaluate performance characteristics including Selectivity, Instrument calibration, Bias/Trueness, Precision, Quantitation limits and range, and Detection limits.

preconditions.text: Before validating an analytical method, it is necessary to ensure that...

steps.text: Validate the analytical method.
Investigate the stability of the analyte(s) from the time of sampling through the time of analysis.
Investigate the extract stability.
Evaluate selectivity by testing appropriate matrix blanks and fortified (spiked) matrix blanks.
Test a worst-case sample matrix during both intra- and interlaboratory validation studies.
Study actual samples of material containing an undetermined amount of native analyte to evaluate method selectivity.
Use multiple analytical procedures during an intralaboratory validation study to substantiate the selectivity of the method being evaluated.

preconditions.text: Ensure that proper sample preservation and storage stability studies were performed during method development.
Adequate representations of matrix blanks cannot be obtained or prepared.

constraints.text: Matrix blanks should contain the chemical species, other than the analyte(s) of interest, which are reasonably expected to be present in an actual sample.

steps.text: Substantiate the selectivity of the method being evaluated using multiple analytical procedures during an intralaboratory validation study.
Thoroughly describe the basis for quantitative selectivity measures.
Demonstrate the characteristics of a calibration function and justification for a selected calibration model during an intralaboratory method validation study.

preconditions.text: If quantitative selectivity measures are used

constraints.text: A combination of procedures or techniques that are based on different measurement principles should be used to increase the likelihood that interferences will be identified.

steps.text: Use a simplified calibration approach including only three calibration standards, single readings of each standard, and the calculation of an average response factor.
Thoroughly study, justify, and document the instrument calibration procedure in a method validation report.
Estimate method bias by measuring materials whose composition is reasonably well known, comparing results to those from at least one alternate method or procedure, or analyzing spiked materials.
Investigate the trueness of the measurement method by comparing the accepted reference value with the level of the measured property.

preconditions.text: Calibration performance characteristics are deemed acceptable

steps.text: Include rigorous evaluations of trueness in EPA method validation studies.
Evaluate trueness at the extremes of the quantitation range stated in the scope of a method, and at regulatory levels.

constraints.text: The reference materials approach introduces the important uncertainty of matrix matching, and the reliability of a bias estimate depends upon the relationship between the composition of the reference material and the samples.
When using the alternate method approach, the reliability of a bias estimate is dependent upon how much is known about the performance characteristics of the alternate method.
The matrix spiking approach introduces uncertainty regarding the behavior of spiked materials, compared to materials containing native analyte.

steps.text: Clearly state the repeatability and reproducibility conditions.
Include the effects of laboratory subsampling when appropriate during precision and trueness evaluations.
Evaluate method trueness and precision at the extremes of the quantitation range.
Verify defined limits by testing materials containing analyte at the claimed LOQ.

preconditions.text: If defined limits, such as those based on specifications for precision, are established.

constraints.text: A quantitation range must include either a regulatory or other type of action level.

steps.text: Identify critical control points in the documented method.
Complete an interlaboratory method validation study prior to publishing an EPA method for general use.
Document any deviations that occur from the interlaboratory study protocol.
Prepare a method validation report suitable for placing in the public docket.

preconditions.text: A successful intralaboratory study has been completed and the issuing entity has demonstrated a pressing programmatic need for publishing a national method.

constraints.text: The fractional factorial experimental design referenced herein is not intended for method optimization purposes.
Deviations from the interlaboratory study protocol should be strongly discouraged.
The report should address the method validation topics outlined in this guidance document.

steps.text: Generate sufficient data to demonstrate and justify the method-recommended calibration approach during method validation.
Evaluate trueness using samples that span the degree of analytical difficulty encompassed by the specified scope and applicability.
Evaluate methods designed for demonstrating compliance with regulatory requirements for both repeatability (within-lab) and reproducibility (among labs).
Characterize analytical measurement uncertainty over a quantitation range.
Verify detection limit estimates derived from mathematical definitions or statistics by analyzing samples.
Verify detection limit estimates derived from mathematical definitions or statistics by analyzing samples containing analyte at the claimed detection level.
Include the design and results of the interlaboratory study in the method validation summary report.
Report data from the interlaboratory study in tabular form.
Include a discussion describing the details of, and rationale for, any changes made to the method resulting from the interlaboratory study.
Peer review EPA methods of analysis in accordance with the EPA Science Technology Policy Council Peer Review Handbook or review by a documented alternate procedure.

preconditions.text: Before publication of EPA methods of analysis
